acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Surface lock APIs implemented
- [ ] CLI lock flows use surface APIs
- [ ] Lock internals no longer exposed to CLI
description:
**Problem**
CLI offline unlock/lock-info reaches into daemon lock internals.
design: Provide typed surface APIs for lock inspection and unlock, returning stable admin output schemas.
title: Add surface store-lock info/unlock APIs returning beads-api output types

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Bin targets compile against new crate layout
- [ ] Legacy paths retained only as temporary shims
- [ ] Runtime behavior unchanged
description:
**Problem**
Binaries currently assume monolithic internal module ownership.
design: Update bin entrypoints to call new crates; shrink `beads-rs` to compatibility/orchestration role.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: binary boundary remains stable while shared repl frame+proto ownership now lives in beads-daemon-core; beads-rs binaries compile against new shared boundary via compatibility shims. Verified cargo check -p beads-rs and --bin tailnet_proxy.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress update: root crate error wiring is now daemon-decoupled (bd-21eg.32 closed), and repl frame+proto ownership moved into beads-daemon-core with beads-rs compatibility shims. This reduces binary coupling and keeps runtime behavior stable while we continue migrating main CLI entrypoint ownership.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: binary/entrypoint wiring remains green after CLI boundary cleanup. crates/beads-rs/src/bin/main.rs continues to call beads_rs::run_cli_entrypoint, and command dispatch now routes prime/setup/onboard through beads-cli-backed handlers without local compatibility modules. Verified with cargo check -p beads-rs --bins and cargo check --workspace; behavior-preserving path.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Validation evidence before close: cargo check -p beads-rs --bins, cargo check --workspace, cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: (6 pass), and daemon/repl integration slices all pass. With prime/setup/onboard dispatch now routed directly through beads-cli-backed handlers and no local wrapper modules, .24 acceptance criteria are satisfied.

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content:
Progress: binary boundary remains stable while shared repl frame+proto ownership now lives in beads-daemon-core; beads-rs binaries compile against new shared boundary via compatibility shims. Verified cargo check -p beads-rs and --bin tailnet_proxy.

Progress update: root crate error wiring is now daemon-decoupled (bd-21eg.32 closed), and repl frame+proto ownership moved into beads-daemon-core with beads-rs compatibility shims. This reduces binary coupling and keeps runtime behavior stable while we continue migrating main CLI entrypoint ownership.

Progress: binary/entrypoint wiring remains green after CLI boundary cleanup. crates/beads-rs/src/bin/main.rs continues to call beads_rs::run_cli_entrypoint, and command dispatch now routes prime/setup/onboard through beads-cli-backed handlers without local compatibility modules. Verified with cargo check -p beads-rs --bins and cargo check --workspace; behavior-preserving path.

Validation evidence before close: cargo check -p beads-rs --bins, cargo check --workspace, cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: (6 pass), and daemon/repl integration slices all pass. With prime/setup/onboard dispatch now routed directly through beads-cli-backed handlers and no local wrapper modules, .24 acceptance criteria are satisfied.

title: Rewire binaries and keep beads-rs as thin orchestration/compat shim
